This kernel appears to be a synthetic/contrived microbenchmark rather than something from a known benchmark suite or tutorial. The specific characteristics — the arbitrary prime-number strides (17, 257), the power-of-two masking (`& 4095`), the hardcoded loop count of 8 with a multiply-accumulate using `1.1f` — look like they were hand-crafted to create a specific memory access pattern and compute-to-memory ratio, possibly for testing cache behavior or as a toy example. It doesn't match any well-known benchmark (e.g., Rodinia, Parboil, SHOC) or standard CUDA tutorial code that I'm aware of.
